provenance.source_corpus: ALTERNATIVE SILVICULTURAL SYSTEMS FOR HARVESTING AND REGENERATING SPRUCE-DOMINATED BOREAL MINERAL WETLANDS

source_spans.text: Mounding, the chosen site preparation, was carried out on both the strip-cut and the patch-cut areas
There has been better survival on mounded than on non-mounded microsites
Seedling growth (especially height growth) 5 years after harvest was better for all species in the strip-cut than the patch-cut
narrow clear-cut strips alternating with uncut strips and a patch clear-cut were tested
seedlings being raised above the anoxic conditions that existed below ground level in 1997
The cutting pattern kept the wind below the threshold for large-scale windthrow
Mounding resulted in less cover, especially for the grass stratum
Neither strip-cutting nor patch-cutting resulted in wetting-up
The presence of treed strips in the strip-cut treatment prevented maximum surface temperatures from reaching the levels attained on the patch-cut
The presence of treed strips in the strip-cut treatment prevented maximum surface temperatures
